note: Venue facts (coordinates, names, addresses, websites, opening hours, tips) from OpenStreetMap via Overpass API — © OpenStreetMap contributors, ODbL 1.0. Editorial takes, vibe tags, energy and price tiers (≈ estimates) are ChiLocal's own opinion. Opening hours reflect OSM data and may be out of date — always linked out for confirmation.

venues.name: 5 Rabanitos
venues.take: A humble room where the mole and chile rellenos outclass places charging triple. BYO energy, bring everyone.

venues.name: Aba
venues.take: A leafy rooftop greenhouse of hummus and cool wine. On a warm night it's the easiest yes in Fulton Market.

venues.name: Adler Planetarium
venues.take: Space shows inside, and the single best skyline panorama in Chicago outside. Evening events here are a criminally underrated date.

venues.name: AIRE Ancient Baths
venues.take: Candlelit thermal pools inside an old factory. The most transporting two hours you can book in January.

venues.name: Alinea
venues.take: Dinner as a magic show — edible balloons, plates painted on the table. For the anniversary you want mythologized.

venues.name: All Together Now
venues.take: A bright little wine shop-café hybrid where the pours are natural and the snacks punch up. Early-evening magic.

venues.name: Al's Beef
venues.take: The Taylor Street original. Order it dipped, lean over the counter, ruin your shirt with honor.

venues.name: Andy's Jazz Club
venues.take: Straight-ahead jazz with dinner service, two sets a night. The easy on-ramp to a jazz habit.

venues.name: Aragon Ballroom
venues.take: A Moorish palace with stars painted on the ceiling. The sound is famously rough; the room forgives everything.

venues.name: Art Institute of Chicago
venues.take: A world-class collection you can wander like a city. Pick one wing, go slow, argue about the Caillebotte over drinks after.

venues.name: Athenian Room
venues.take: Kalamata chicken and Greek fries that have fueled generations of DePaul dates. Cheap, fast, weirdly perfect.

venues.name: Avec
venues.take: A cedar sauna of a dining room that changed how Chicago eats. Squeeze in late for bacon-wrapped dates and strangers-turned-friends.

venues.name: Bar Roma
venues.take: Cacio e pepe and negronis with Andersonville warmth. The patio out back feels like a Roman courtyard that took the Blue Line.

venues.name: Beat Kitchen
venues.take: A shoebox rock room where next year's favorite band plays to eighty people. Cheap tickets, close stage.

venues.name: Beatnik
venues.take: Antique mirrors, palms, and cocktails that arrive like theater props. Maximalism done with a straight face.

venues.name: Begyle Brewing
venues.take: A no-frills family taproom on Ravenswood's brewery corridor. Bring a pizza in, make friends at the long tables.

venues.name: Bernice's Tavern
venues.take: A century-old family dive with bingo nights and buckets of character. The South Side at its most welcoming.

venues.name: Big Chicks
venues.take: An art-covered queer bar that feels like a very good house party. Everyone's welcome; the wall art alone is worth the trip.

venues.name: Big Star
venues.take: Tacos, bourbon, and the patio that defines Chicago summer. Weeknights are the move; weekends are a sport.

venues.name: Billy Sunday
venues.take: Amari, obscure bottles, and bartenders who love a question. A drink here feels like a short seminar you actually enjoy.

venues.name: Birrieria Zaragoza
venues.take: One family, one dish: La Barca-style goat birria, done to perfection for decades. A pilgrimage that costs fifteen dollars.

venues.name: Buddy Guy's Legends
venues.take: The living legend's own club, with blues seven nights and gumbo on the menu. In January, the man himself takes the stage.

venues.name: Cabra
venues.take: Ceviche and pisco thirteen floors up, under string lights. Summer-night energy bottled on a rooftop.

venues.name: La Scarola
venues.take: Chaotic, garlicky, photo-covered red-sauce joint where regulars are family. Order the escarole and let the room happen to you.

venues.name: California Clipper
venues.take: A 1937 lounge with curved booths and live twang most nights. Feels like a movie set that serves a great Old Fashioned.

venues.name: Carol's Pub
venues.take: A honky-tonk on the North Side with live country bands and two-stepping strangers. Unbeatable at 1 a.m.

venues.name: Celeste
venues.take: Three floors from Victorian parlor to garden roof, with dancing creeping in as the night goes. One address, an entire evening arc.

venues.name: Chicago Diner
venues.take: Meat-free since before it was cool, and the milkshakes convert skeptics. Comfort food with a conscience.

venues.name: Chicago Magic Lounge
venues.take: Enter through a laundromat, end up in an Art Deco theater with close-up magic at your table. Pure date-night alchemy.

venues.name: Chief O'Neill's
venues.take: A proper Irish pub with trad sessions and one of the best beer gardens on the Northwest Side. Go for the music, stay for the roast.

venues.name: Ciccio Mio
venues.take: A candlelit time machine to 1940s Italian-American dining. The kind of room that makes you order a second negroni.

venues.name: Cindy's
venues.take: A glasshouse rooftop staring straight down at the Bean and Millennium Park. Sunset here converts even the rooftop-skeptical.

venues.name: Clark Street Ale House
venues.nameOsm: Clark Street Ale House - River North
venues.take: A 'STOP & DRINK' sign, midwestern taps, and a beer garden hiding out back. River North's least pretentious room by a mile.

venues.name: Club Lucky
venues.take: Red sauce, martinis, and a 1940s supper-club room that hasn't changed because it never needed to.

venues.name: Cole's
venues.take: Free live music and comedy in the back, cheap beer up front. The lowest-stakes great night out in the neighborhood.

venues.name: Constellation
venues.take: A listening room for jazz and new music where the audience actually listens. Sit close; let it get strange.

venues.name: Coq d'Or
venues.take: The Drake's wood-paneled bar, pouring since the day after Prohibition ended. Order a Manhattan and let the leather booth do its work.

venues.name: Court Theatre
venues.take: Tony-winning classic theater on the UChicago campus — Greek tragedy to August Wilson, staged with rigor. Ideas-first date night.

venues.name: Symphony Center
venues.take: One of the world's great orchestras, and cheap gallery seats if you're flexible. Dress up a little; let the hall do the rest.

venues.name: Chicago Cultural Center
venues.take: Free exhibitions under the largest Tiffany dome on earth. The most beautiful zero-dollar hour in Chicago — go before it closes at night.

venues.name: Daisies
venues.take: Midwest vegetables and handmade pasta treated like the main event. Gentle, pretty, quietly excellent.

venues.name: Davenport's Piano Bar
venues.take: A proper cabaret room where singers work the standards and the piano bar takes requests. Delightfully out of step with everything.

venues.name: Davis Theater
venues.take: A century-old neighborhood cinema with a full bar attached. Movie, then a nightcap next door without moving the car.

venues.name: Demera
venues.take: Injera, honey wine, and a corner view of the Uptown marquees. Eating with your hands is the icebreaker.

venues.name: Dovetail Brewery
venues.take: Continental lagers brewed the slow old-world way, poured in a hall that feels lifted from Bavaria. The Maibock season is an event.

venues.name: The Duck Inn
venues.take: A working-class corner turned duck-fat-everything tavern. The rotisserie duck for two is the neighborhood's best date order.

venues.name: East Room
venues.take: No sign, no cover most nights, DJs until very late. The dance-floor answer when you didn't plan on dancing.

venues.name: Emporium Arcade Bar
venues.take: Craft beer and a wall of vintage cabinets. Settle it over Street Fighter II like adults.

venues.name: Empty Bottle
venues.take: Scrappy, beloved, and booking the best weird rock in town for thirty years. Free Mondays are a gateway drug.

venues.name: Epiphany Center for the Arts
venues.take: Concerts and art shows inside a restored 1885 church — stained glass, stone arches, full bar. The room upstages most headliners.

venues.name: Estereo
venues.take: All-day Latin American bar that turns into a low-key dance floor after dark. Agave, cumbia, windows open in summer.

venues.name: Exchequer Restaurant & Pub
venues.take: Deep dish and ribs in a former Capone speakeasy, walls crammed with a century of memorabilia. Pre-theater fuel with a wink.

venues.name: Fireside Bowl
venues.take: A punk-rock bowling alley that survived every era of the neighborhood. Scuffed lanes, loud speakers, perfect.

venues.name: The Friendship
venues.take: A modern supper club with jazz combos and a menu that respects the classics. Dinner-and-a-show without leaving the room.

venues.name: Garden of the Phoenix
venues.take: A Japanese garden on a Jackson Park island, with Yoko Ono's lotus sculpture rising by the water. Serenity hiding behind the museum.

venues.name: Garfield Park Conservatory
venues.take: Two acres of jungle under glass — 75 degrees and green when the city is grey. February's secret weapon; check evening event nights.

venues.name: Geja's Cafe
venues.take: Candlelit fondue and live flamenco guitar since the '60s. Corny? Absolutely. Also the most reliable romance in the city.

venues.name: Gene Siskel Film Center
venues.take: Restorations, premieres, and films you won't stream for years. The programming does the deciding for you.

venues.name: Giant
venues.take: Tiny room, huge flavors, zero pretension. The kind of place you leave already planning the next visit.

venues.name: Gilt Bar
venues.take: Dark wood, dim lamps, ricotta gnocchi you'll dream about. River North without the River North energy.

venues.name: Girl & the Goat
venues.take: Still loud, still fun, still worth it after all these years. Share everything; fight over the pig face.

venues.name: GMan Tavern
venues.take: Metro's kid-sibling bar: good jukebox, pinball, pre- and post-show energy without the ticket.

venues.name: Golden Dagger
venues.take: A little indie listening room with coffee, cocktails, and bands on the way up. Low cover, high hit rate.

venues.name: Green Door Tavern
venues.take: A crooked 1872 building that's been pouring since Prohibition (ask about the speakeasy downstairs). Burgers and history in equal measure.

venues.name: Green Mill
venues.take: Velvet booths, a century of jazz, and a no-phones glare that keeps it holy. The single most Chicago room in Chicago — bring cash.

venues.name: HaiSous
venues.take: Vietnamese cooking with fire and finesse — clay-pot fish, wings you'll defend with your life. Pilsen's proudest dining room.

venues.name: Half Acre Beer Company
venues.nameOsm: Half Acre Beer Co. Balmoral Tap Room & Garden
venues.take: The Daisy Cutter mothership — a big, friendly Balmoral taproom with a garden out back. Where beer-nerd and neighborhood-regular overlap.

venues.name: The Hideout
venues.take: A century-old balloon-frame house hiding between factories, booking country, poetry, and everything odd and wonderful. Chicago's clubhouse.

venues.name: Honey Butter Fried Chicken
venues.take: Fried chicken with corn muffins and zero irony. Counter service, patio out back, everyone leaves happy.

venues.name: Hopleaf
venues.take: Mussels, frites, and a Belgian beer list you could study for years. The back room is one of the city's great winter dens.

venues.name: Humboldt Park Boathouse
venues.take: A Prairie-style boathouse on a lagoon with skyline peeking over the trees. Paddleboats in summer, postcard light at dusk.

venues.name: Hungry Brain
venues.take: Thrift-store couches and some of the most adventurous jazz in the city. Sunday improv sets are a rite of passage.

venues.name: Ina Mae Tavern
venues.take: Po'boys and hurricanes done with real care. The closest Chicago gets to a French Quarter porch.

venues.name: Italian Village
venues.take: Upstairs at the Village: twinkling lights, private booths, and red sauce served since 1927. Kitsch aged into genuine romance.

venues.name: Jazz Showcase
venues.take: A listening room in the old Dearborn Station where the music is the whole point. Seventy years of serious bookings, zero pretense.

venues.name: Jim's Original
venues.take: Polish sausage, grilled onions, and free hot peppers at 3 a.m., same as it's been since Maxwell Street days. The city's great equalizer.

venues.name: Kasama
venues.take: Filipino cooking that runs from breakfast pastries to a full tasting menu. One of the most exciting kitchens in the city, full stop.

venues.name: Kie-Gol-Lanee
venues.take: Oaxacan moles and tlayudas from a family kitchen that deserves triple the fame. Order the mole negro and go quiet for a minute.

venues.name: Kimski
venues.take: Korean-Polish street food from the Maria's family — kimchi on your pierogi and it works. Only in Bridgeport.

venues.name: Kingston Mines
venues.take: Two stages of sweaty, no-frills blues until deep into the night. Tourists come, locals stay — the music doesn't care.

venues.name: Kuma's Corner
venues.take: Burgers named after metal bands, served loud. Not a quiet date — that's the point.

venues.name: La Chaparrita
venues.take: A grocery-store taqueria slinging some of the city's best suadero and tripas. Trust the salsa bar with your life.

venues.name: Lao Peng You
venues.take: Hand-pulled noodles and dumplings from two brothers who treat dough like a craft. Small menu, no wrong answers.

venues.name: Le Bouchon
venues.take: Twenty seats of unapologetic French bistro — duck, candlelight, elbows almost touching. Romance without the fuss.

venues.name: Lem's Bar-B-Q
venues.take: Aquarium-smoker rib tips and hot links that define South Side barbecue. Takeout only — eat on the hood of the car like you're supposed to.

venues.name: Alfred Caldwell Lily Pool
venues.take: A Prairie School stone lagoon hiding in plain sight off Fullerton. Most Chicagoans have never found it; now you have.

venues.name: Lincoln Park Conservatory
venues.take: A Victorian fern paradise next to the free zoo. Small, warm, and quietly transporting — pair with a lakefront walk.

venues.name: Lincoln Hall
venues.take: Arguably the best-sounding mid-size room in the city. If a band you half-know is playing, that's the plan.

venues.name: The Book Cellar
venues.take: A bookstore that pours wine while you browse. Lincoln Square's coziest first-stop before dinner on Giddings Plaza.

venues.name: Logan Arcade
venues.take: Row after row of lovingly maintained pinball. Bring quarters and a competitive streak; lose track of two hours.

venues.name: Longman & Eagle
venues.take: A roadside inn with a whiskey wall and serious cooking. Walk-ins only — come hungry, stay for one more pour.

venues.name: The Loyalist
venues.take: The clubby downstairs sibling of Smyth — dim, buzzy, and home to a burger people argue about nationally. Late seatings reward the patient.

venues.name: Lula Cafe
venues.take: The neighborhood farm-to-table that started it all — seasonal, unhurried, and still the safest great-night bet in Logan Square.

venues.name: Maggie Daley Park
venues.take: In winter the skating ribbon snakes between the towers — skating a loop through downtown light is peak Chicago romance.

venues.name: Map Room
venues.take: A travelers' tavern papered in maps with one of the city's great beer lists. Come early, claim the corner table.

venues.name: Maria's Packaged Goods & Community Bar
venues.take: A liquor-store-front, bar-in-back 'slashie' that became the South Side's cultural embassy. Start or end every Bridgeport night here.

venues.name: Mario's Italian Lemonade
venues.take: A summer-only lemonade shack that's been an institution since 1954. The line is part of the ritual; the watermelon ice is the reward.

venues.name: Marty's Martini Bar
venues.take: A closet-sized room pouring oversized martinis. Squeeze in, order a classic, act like it's 1962.

venues.name: Museum of Contemporary Art
venues.take: Big-swing contemporary shows a block off Michigan Ave. Tuesday evenings often run late and cheap — the thinking person's happy hour.

venues.name: Metro
venues.take: Four decades of career-making rock shows in a room that still feels like a secret club. Check who's on tonight.

venues.name: Mi Tocaya Antojería
venues.take: Personal, loud-hearted Mexican cooking that swings between comfort and surprise. Order beyond the things you recognize.

venues.name: Middle Brow Bungalow
venues.take: Naturally leavened pizza and house beer in a sunlit corner room. Casual on purpose, careful underneath.

venues.name: Millennium Park
venues.take: The Bean, the Pritzker lawn, and free summer concerts under the skyline. Pack a blanket; check the summer film and music calendar.

venues.name: Miller's Pub
venues.take: Ribs and Manhattans under the L tracks until the small hours. The Loop's dependable old friend.

venues.name: MingHin Cuisine
venues.take: The reliable giant of Chinatown — dim sum by day, lacquered ducks and clay pots late. Bring a crowd and order wide.

venues.name: Monteverde
venues.take: Pasta made in the window, cacio whey pepe on the table. Book ahead or charm the bar — either way, go.

venues.name: Montrose Beach
venues.take: The city's biggest beach, plus dunes, birds, and a bar on the sand. Stay for the skyline turning pink over the water.

venues.name: Moody Tongue
venues.take: A brewery with Michelin ambitions — beer pairings treated like grand cru. The strangest fancy night in town, in the best way.

venues.name: Music Box Theatre
venues.take: A 1929 movie palace with clouds on the ceiling and a real organ. Any film here becomes an occasion — check the late shows.

venues.name: Myopic Books
venues.take: Three creaking floors of used books, open late. The rare first-date test both people can pass.

venues.name: National Museum of Mexican Art
venues.take: Free, gorgeous, and the beating heart of Pilsen — folk art to contemporary, plus the city's best Día de Muertos show. Go before dinner.

venues.name: The Neo-Futurists
venues.take: Thirty plays in sixty minutes, order set by the audience, price set by a dice roll. The best late-night theater experiment in America.

venues.name: North Avenue Beach
venues.take: Volleyball, the boathouse deck, and the classic skyline-from-the-sand view. Chicago summer's front porch.

venues.name: North Pond
venues.take: An Arts-and-Crafts pavilion on a pond, skyline over the reeds. The most scenic tasting menu in the city.

venues.name: Northerly Island
venues.take: A prairie loop on the lake with the skyline stacked behind the water. Bring a picnic; watch the planes and sailboats trade places.

venues.name: Old Town Ale House
venues.take: Scandalous paintings, a jukebox of standards, and bartenders who've seen everything. Roger Ebert called it the best bar in the world; who are we to argue.

venues.name: Milton Lee Olive Park
venues.take: A tree-lined promenade jutting into the lake beside Navy Pier that somehow nobody knows. Skyline, water, quiet — a hidden-in-plain-sight stunner.

venues.name: Oriole
venues.take: Through a freight elevator into one of America's warmest fine-dining rooms. Special-occasion cooking without the stiffness.

venues.name: Osito's Tap
venues.take: A speakeasy behind a candy shop pouring mezcal with pride and skill. La Villita's best-kept not-so-secret.

venues.name: Oz Park
venues.take: A neighborhood park guarded by statues of Dorothy, the Tin Man, and friends. A gentle stroll with a wink — pair it with ice cream.

venues.name: Palmisano Park
venues.take: A limestone quarry turned hilltop park with a skyline view most rooftops would kill for. Golden hour here is a cheat code.

venues.name: Pequod's Pizza
venues.take: Caramelized-crust pan pizza with a cult that grows by the year. Order ahead or embrace the wait with a pitcher.

venues.name: Phyllis' Musical Inn
venues.take: A former polka hall with the neighborhood's best-hidden beer garden and bands that play for the door. Division Street's soul survivor.

venues.name: 16th Street murals
venues.take: Blocks of murals along 16th Street's rail embankment — an open-air gallery that changes every season. Walk it, then eat in the neighborhood.

venues.name: Ping Tom Memorial Park
venues.take: A riverfront park with pagoda pavilions and trains rumbling across the water. Chinatown's quiet showstopper at dusk.

venues.name: Pizza Lobo
venues.take: Foldable New York slices, frozen drinks, and a patio that hums all summer. Zero occasion required.

venues.name: Podhalanka
venues.take: The last old-Polonia diner standing — pierogi and borscht served like your babcia is watching. Cash, and go before it's gone.

venues.name: Pompei
venues.take: Taylor Street's century-old bakery-café. Get the strudel pizza and a table by the window before a Little Italy stroll.

venues.name: Pops for Champagne
venues.take: A round bar devoted entirely to bubbles, decades before that was a trend. Celebrate something — or invent something to celebrate.

venues.name: Promontory Point
venues.take: Limestone steps into the lake and the full skyline floating to the north. The most romantic free real estate in Chicago.

venues.name: The Publican
venues.take: A beer-hall cathedral to pork, oysters, and Belgian ale. Communal tables, zero quiet, maximum joy.

venues.name: Punch House
venues.take: A wood-paneled '70s basement pouring communal punch bowls under a fish tank. Bring three friends and split a bowl.

venues.name: Qing Xiang Yuan Dumpling
venues.take: Handmade dumplings in flavors from classic to curious, folded to order. Cheap, fast, quietly perfect.

venues.name: Rainbo Club
venues.take: The artists' dive: cheap drinks, a photo booth, and more Chicago history per stool than anywhere on Division.

venues.name: Ramova Theatre
venues.take: A 1929 neighborhood palace brought back from thirty years of dust — shows, a brewery, and Bridgeport pride in every seat.

venues.name: Reggies
venues.take: Punk and metal most nights, rooftop grill upstairs, zero attitude about any of it. The South Loop's loudest good time.

venues.name: Driehaus Museum
venues.take: A marble-and-onyx Gilded Age palace frozen in 1883. Wander it slowly and feel wildly underdressed in the best way.

venues.name: Richard's Bar
venues.take: Sinatra on the speakers, Malört on the rail, decades in the wood paneling. An old-school Chicago bar experience, no theme-park version.

venues.name: Ricobene's
venues.take: Home of the breaded-steak sandwich — a gloriously excessive Chicago original. Napkins are not optional.

venues.name: Chicago Riverwalk
venues.take: A mile and a half of river, wine kiosks, and architecture lit up overhead. The city's best free evening stroll.

venues.name: Riviera Theatre
venues.take: A faded-gold former movie palace that makes every show feel bigger. Check the marquee before you plan anything else.

venues.name: Build Coffee
venues.take: A bookstore-café inside the Experimental Station — zines, used paperbacks, and serious neighborhood roots. Daytime-into-dusk energy.

venues.name: Rootstock
venues.take: Natural wine and a kitchen that outperforms the room's humility. Late pours for the after-something crowd.

venues.name: Rosa's Lounge
venues.take: A family-run blues room far from the tourist strip, where the players come to actually play. Mama Rosa's legacy is the real thing.

venues.name: Rose Mary
venues.take: Croatian-Italian 'Adriatic drinking food' with serious fire behind it. Book early; the room earns its hype.

venues.name: The Royal Palms Shuffleboard Club
venues.take: A warehouse of full-size shuffleboard courts, tropical drinks, and instant friendly rivalry. The easiest 'we did something' night there is.

venues.name: The Salt Shed
venues.take: The old Morton Salt factory reborn as the city's most striking concert hall — riverside, neon-crowned, instantly iconic. Summer shows outside are magic.

venues.name: San Soo Gab San
venues.take: Charcoal Korean BBQ with a table full of banchan at hours when almost nothing else is open. The 1 a.m. feast of champions.

venues.name: Schubas Tavern
venues.take: A wood-paneled back room built for songwriters. Intimate enough to hear the lyrics, tavern up front for after.

venues.name: Scofflaw
venues.take: Gin drinks, a fireplace, and armchairs you sink into. Built for the night the wind comes off the lake sideways.

venues.name: The Second City
venues.take: The comedy factory that built SNL's bench. The mainstage revue is the safe bet; the late-night improv sets are the loose cannon.

venues.name: Sidetrack
venues.take: Showtunes night at this sprawling video bar is a Chicago institution — a thousand people belting Sondheim. Surrender to it.

venues.name: Simon's Tavern
venues.take: A Prohibition-era Swedish dive that serves warm glögg all winter. The neon fish sign alone deserves a landmark plaque.

venues.name: Skylark
venues.take: Tater tots, stiff pours, a photo booth, cash only. The Pilsen dive every other dive quietly measures itself against.

venues.name: Sleeping Village
venues.take: Tap wall up front, great bookings in back, string lights over the patio. The rare venue that's also just a good bar.

venues.name: The Slippery Slope
venues.take: Skee-ball up front, a packed no-cover dance floor in back. Sweaty, unserious, reliably fun past midnight.

venues.name: Smartbar
venues.take: The city's serious dance floor since the '80s — world-class DJs, zero bottle service. Go after midnight or don't go.

venues.name: International Museum of Surgical Science
venues.take: Four floors of antique surgical tools and murals in a lakefront mansion. Macabre, fascinating, and a guaranteed conversation generator.

venues.name: Sound-Bar
venues.take: A proper multi-room club with a serious system when you want lasers-and-bass maximalism. Dress code applies; naps beforehand advised.

venues.name: Spacca Napoli
venues.take: Pizza napoletana made with near-religious devotion — the owner studied in Naples and it shows in every blistered crust.

venues.name: Steppenwolf Theatre
venues.take: The ensemble that put Chicago acting on the map, still swinging hard. Check what's on; rush seats reward the spontaneous.

venues.name: Stony Island Arts Bank
venues.take: A rescued 1920s bank turned archive of Black culture — Frankie Knuckles' records, Johnson Publishing's library. Unlike anywhere else in America.

venues.name: Subterranean
venues.take: A tall skinny room at the six corners where you're never more than forty feet from the band.

venues.name: Sultan's Market
venues.take: Falafel sandwiches and lentil soup that have rescued a thousand budgets. Order at the counter, grab the window ledge.

venues.name: Sun Wah BBQ
venues.take: Ducks in the window, the Beijing duck feast on the table. Order it, watch the carving, thank yourself later.

venues.name: Superdawg
venues.take: A 1948 drive-in with flirting hot dogs on the roof and carhops at your window. Milkshakes, neon, pure Americana.

venues.name: Thalia Hall
venues.take: An 1892 opera house modeled on Prague's, now booking the best mid-size shows in the city. Balcony seats feel like time travel.

venues.name: Bloomingdale Trail
venues.take: An old rail line turned 2.7-mile elevated promenade over the Northwest Side. Walk it at dusk, drop down wherever dinner smells best.

venues.name: Charleston
venues.take: A warm corner tavern with occasional bluegrass in the window and no screens worth watching. Conversation wins here.

venues.name: The Promontory
venues.take: Hearth-fired dinners downstairs, concerts and dance parties upstairs. Hyde Park's all-in-one night out.

venues.name: Three Dots and a Dash
venues.take: Down an alley, under the skulls, into a rum-soaked grotto. Order the flaming bowl; make the night ridiculous.

venues.name: Timber Lanes
venues.take: Eight lanes, paper scoring, a cash bar, and pins set by hand like it's 1945. Bowling stripped to its charming essentials.

venues.name: Twin Anchors
venues.take: Ribs in a 1930s tavern where Sinatra held court. No reservations — put your name in and have an Old Style.

venues.name: Vincent
venues.take: Moules-frites and gin in a dark-wooded corner bistro. The late-ish kitchen makes it the neighborhood's best second act.

venues.name: Virtue
venues.take: Southern cooking with white-tablecloth care and South Side soul. The cauliflower and short rib are already classics.

venues.name: Whirlaway Lounge
venues.take: A living-room dive where the jukebox and the regulars do the decorating. Cheap, warm, and utterly itself.

venues.name: Women & Children First
venues.take: A feminist bookstore that's anchored Andersonville for decades — readings, staff picks with teeth, browsing that turns into hours.

venues.name: Zanies Comedy Club
venues.take: A low-ceilinged classic where touring headliners work close enough to see you check your phone. Don't.

venues.name: Zebra Lounge
venues.take: A tiny zebra-striped piano bar that's been enabling singalongs since 1929. Absurd, intimate, unkillable.
